title: Thomas Cup Squad Reassembles for Asian Games: Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: This story details the Indian badminton squad for the Asian Games, highlighting the return of key players like Treesa-Gayatri and the inclusion of top stars.
summary: The Indian Thomas Cup squad is reassembling for the Asian Games, with Treesa-Gayatri returning to the lineup alongside other prominent players like PV Sindhu, Lakshya, Unnati, and Satwik-Chirag.

title: Is Tanvi Sharma's Game Like Saina Nehwal's or PV Sindhu's?
indian_angle: This article analyzes the playing style of rising Indian shuttler Tanvi Sharma, comparing it to Indian legends Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu.
summary: An analysis delves into whether young Indian shuttler Tanvi Sharma's game development aligns more with the aggressive style of Saina Nehwal or the defensive prowess of PV Sindhu.

title: Indian Shuttlers' FIFA World Cup Picks: Chirag-Prannoy for Argentina
indian_angle: This piece shares the FIFA World Cup preferences of top Indian badminton players like Chirag, Prannoy, Ayush, and Sindhu.
summary: Top Indian badminton players, including Chirag, Prannoy, Ayush, and Sindhu, reveal their favorite teams for the FIFA World Cup 2026, with Chirag and Prannoy backing Argentina.

title: Coach Tan Kim Her Paired Hari-Arjun After Satwik-Chirag Success
indian_angle: This story provides insight into how Indian doubles coach Tan Kim Her strategically formed the Hari-Arjun pair, following the success of Satwik-Chirag.
summary: Indian doubles coach Tan Kim Her discusses his strategy behind forming the new Hari-Arjun pair, drawing parallels and lessons from the successful partnership of Satwik-Chirag.

title: Ayush Shetty Gives No Respite to World No 15 Weng Hong Yang
indian_angle: This report highlights Indian player Ayush Shetty's strong performance against a higher-ranked Chinese opponent.
summary: Indian shuttler Ayush Shetty delivered a relentless performance, giving no respite to Chinese World No. 15 Weng Hong Yang in their recent encounter.

title: Sindhu, Arjun-Hariharan Advance to Round 2 at Indonesia Open
indian_angle: This news reports on the successful progression of Indian stars PV Sindhu and the pair of Arjun-Hariharan into the second round of the Indonesia Open.
summary: Indian shuttlers PV Sindhu and the men's doubles pair of Arjun and Hariharan successfully advanced to the second round of the Indonesia Open.
